name: Separation by Distant Placement
strauss_formulation: to speak of the same subject in a contradictory manner on pages far apart from each other
mechanism: Two incompatible statements concerning the same subject are separated within a work so that ordinary reading may fail to hold them together.
documentary_function: Direct bounded documentary attention under LC-001 without authorizing a concealed-teaching inference.
investigative_requirement: Compare statements concerning the same subject across the whole work rather than treating local context as the complete evidentiary field.
distinguished_from: LC-001 concerns positional separation; it does not by itself concern unequal prominence, which belongs to LC-002.
LC-001 requires a contradiction between propositions, not merely a thematic tension or difference of emphasis.
Distance is an attention-obscuring condition, not proof of concealment or proof of authorial intention.
No fixed numerical page threshold is constitutive; Strauss states that careless reading permits any positive separation to function this way.
minimum_trigger_features: Two distinct statement records occur in the same work.
Both statements concern the same reconstructed subject.
The propositions are mutually incompatible after explicit reconstruction.
The statements occupy positively separated positions in the work.
required_corroboration_features: Additional statements on the same subject reproduce or sharpen the incompatibility.
Exact or controlled lexical recurrence links the separated passages.
The author explicitly claims exceptional care in composition or awareness of contradictions.
Placement, sequence, omission, repetition, or another independently reconstructed device connects the passages.
A source explicitly identified by the author clarifies that the contradiction is deliberate or pedagogically governed.
ordinary_alternatives: The statements concern different senses of the same term.
One statement is conditional, qualified, hypothetical, dialectical, ironic, or reported rather than asserted.
The work records development or a genuine change of position over time.
The statements address different audiences, circumstances, cases, or levels of generality.
One statement belongs to a different speaker or cited authority.
A textual variant, translation problem, transcription error, or corrupt witness creates the appearance of contradiction.
The contradiction is an ordinary mistake rather than a literary operation.
disqualifying_conditions: The statements do not concern the same subject.
The propositions are compatible once terms, scope, time, condition, or attribution are reconstructed.
The two records are duplicate witnesses of one statement rather than distinct statements.
Source integrity or speaker identity cannot be established.
The alleged contradiction is inferred solely from topic similarity or embedding distance.
Only one statement is recoverable.
authorized_engine_action: Register the pair as a noncanonical development candidate. Recover both passages and their full local contexts. Reconstruct each statement independently before comparison. Test all ordinary alternatives and preserve unresolved ones. Search the whole work for additional statements on the same subject. Seek independent corroborating indicators. Open a bounded interpretive inquiry only after the documentary trigger survives adversarial testing. Terminate with underdetermination when the evidence cannot distinguish competing explanations.
prohibited_inferences: Do not infer that the author intentionally concealed a teaching from contradiction and distance alone.
Do not infer that a hidden teaching exists merely because two statements conflict.
Do not select the rarer, later, less prominent, or more radical statement as true under LC-001 alone.
Do not attribute private intention, fear of persecution, audience manipulation, or esotericism without separate evidence.
Do not merge LC-001 with LC-002 or any other technique.
Do not present a semantic-search match or graph proximity as documentary evidence.
uncertainty_note: Until source integrity, subject identity, contradiction, context, attribution, ordinary alternatives, and corroboration are recorded, the engine must preserve the pair only as a candidate and may not characterize it as concealed teaching.

name: Incidental Placement of One Contradictory Statement
strauss_formulation: to make one of the two contradictory statements in passing, as it were
mechanism: One contradictory statement receives conspicuous or extended presentation while the other appears incidentally, parenthetically, in passing, or as an apparently subordinate remark, allowing unequal prominence to obscure their incompatibility.
documentary_function: Direct bounded documentary attention under LC-002 without authorizing a concealed-teaching inference.
investigative_requirement: Index incidental and subordinate remarks and compare them with prominent declarations concerning the same reconstructed subject, while documenting the textual basis for the prominence distinction.
distinguished_from: LC-002 concerns unequal prominence; LC-001 concerns positional separation. Distance is neither necessary nor sufficient for LC-002.
Incidentality is a documented rhetorical or compositional relation, not a synonym for rarity, brevity, low semantic similarity, or low retrieval score.
LC-002 identifies an attention asymmetry. It does not establish that the incidental statement is true, private, superior, or authorially preferred.
A parenthesis, qualification, example, objection, or reported view is not automatically an incidental contradiction; its proposition and voice must be independently reconstructed.
LC-018 and LC-019 concern frequency and a separate rule of discernment. They must not be imported into LC-002 silently.
minimum_trigger_features: Two distinct statement records occur in the same work.
Both statements concern the same reconstructed subject.
The propositions are mutually incompatible after explicit reconstruction.
One statement is placed incidentally, parenthetically, in passing, or as a subordinate remark.
The other statement receives demonstrably greater textual, rhetorical, or architectonic prominence.
required_corroboration_features: The incidental proposition recurs in other low-prominence or carefully qualified locations.
Exact or controlled lexical recurrence links the incidental and prominent statements.
The work repeatedly foregrounds one proposition while preserving the contrary proposition in subordinate placement.
Audience markers, direct address, headings, omissions, sequence, or another independently reconstructed technique reinforces the asymmetry.
The author explicitly claims exceptional compositional care or provides a rule requiring attention to passing remarks.
Recovery of a cited source shows that the incidental statement carries a precise qualification obscured by the prominent account.
ordinary_alternatives: The incidental statement is merely an example, exception, qualification, or special case compatible with the prominent statement.
The statement belongs to an objection, quotation, hypothetical case, dialectical stage, or different speaker rather than the author's asserted position.
The apparent asymmetry follows from genre, syntax, editorial layout, translation, or witness formatting rather than authorial composition.
The prominent statement is a general rule and the incidental statement a legitimate limitation within its scope.
Different meanings of a shared term create the appearance of contradiction.
A textual variant, transcription error, corrupt witness, or mistranslation produces the conflict or the apparent incidentality.
The contradiction is an ordinary mistake or unresolved inconsistency rather than a literary operation.
disqualifying_conditions: The statements do not concern the same subject.
The propositions are compatible after scope, condition, attribution, time, or terminology is reconstructed.
No defensible textual basis distinguishes incidental from prominent placement.
The alleged incidental statement is supplied only by paraphrase, inference, search ranking, or graph proximity.
The two records are duplicate witnesses of one statement rather than distinct statements.
Source integrity or voice attribution cannot be established.
Only one statement is recoverable.
authorized_engine_action: Register the pair as a noncanonical development candidate. Recover both passages and their full rhetorical, syntactic, and architectonic contexts. Reconstruct each statement independently before comparison. Document the basis for assigning incidental and prominent roles. Test all ordinary alternatives and preserve unresolved ones. Search the whole work for every recurrence of both propositions and comparable placements. Evaluate related techniques separately rather than treating LC-002 as a general concealment detector. Open a bounded interpretive inquiry only after the contradiction and prominence asymmetry survive adversarial testing. Terminate with underdetermination when evidence cannot distinguish competing explanations.
prohibited_inferences: Do not infer deliberate concealment from unequal prominence alone.
Do not infer that the incidental statement is true, private, unconventional, or authorially preferred under LC-002 alone.
Do not infer that the prominent statement is false or merely exoteric.
Do not treat brevity, rarity, parenthesis, or low prominence as sufficient evidence of doctrinal significance.
Do not import LC-018's frequency rule or any truth-selection rule into LC-002 without separate evaluation.
Do not merge LC-002 with LC-001 or another technique merely because both occur in the same pair.
Do not attribute persecution, audience manipulation, irony, or secret intention without separate evidence.
Do not present semantic-search ranking or graph proximity as documentary evidence.
uncertainty_note: Until source integrity, subject identity, contradiction, contexts, attribution, and the textual basis of unequal prominence are documented and ordinary alternatives tested, the engine must preserve the pair only as a candidate and may not characterize either statement as the concealed teaching.

name: Contradiction of Implications Rather Than Direct Statement
strauss_formulation: to contradict the first statement not directly, but by contradicting its implications
mechanism: A first statement is not verbally denied. One or more additional statements establish a bridge from it to an unpronounced implication, and another explicit statement contradicts that implication. The contradiction to the first statement is therefore recoverable only by reconstructing and validating the intervening logical relation.
documentary_function: Direct bounded documentary attention under LC-003 without authorizing a concealed-teaching inference.
investigative_requirement: Reconstruct each pronounced proposition independently, record every bridge premise, derive the unpronounced implication through an explicit and reviewable rule, and test whether the contrary statement truly denies that implication without changes of term, scope, condition, time, or voice.
distinguished_from: LC-003 concerns indirect logical contradiction; LC-001 concerns distance and LC-002 concerns prominence. Neither distance nor unequal prominence establishes LC-003.
The bracketed propositions in Strauss's symbol are reconstructed implications, not authorial statements. The engine must label them as derived and preserve their complete provenance.
The technique requires a valid implication bridge. Thematic association, semantic similarity, graph connectivity, or shared vocabulary is not a logical bridge.
Apparent identity among terms must be verified. Equivocation, technical shifts, scope changes, and category differences can invalidate the chain.
LC-003 detects an indirect contradiction structure only. It does not determine which statement is true or whether concealment was deliberate.
A chain may contain more than one bridge proposition, but each step must be separately recoverable, attributed, and validated.
minimum_trigger_features: An anchor statement, one or more bridge statements, and a contrary statement occur in the same work.
The selected contrary statement does not directly deny the anchor statement.
The bridge statements connect the anchor proposition to a derived implication.
The derived implication is not itself pronounced in the selected evidence path.
The contrary statement explicitly denies or is incompatible with the derived implication.
required_corroboration_features: The work elsewhere explicitly defines or equates the terms used in the implication bridge.
The same implication chain recurs in another passage or independently recoverable formulation.
A direct contradiction elsewhere confirms the same conflict without replacing the LC-003 analysis.
The author supplies a rule, example, or structural cue requiring the reader to reconstruct unpronounced implications.
Source recovery confirms that the bridge relation was not introduced by translation, paraphrase, or editorial compression.
Another independently reconstructed technique draws attention to the same passages while remaining separately evaluated.
ordinary_alternatives: A shared word changes meaning across the chain, producing equivocation rather than implication.
The anchor or bridge proposition has a narrower scope, condition, time, modality, or domain than the derived implication.
The bridge relation is not transitive or otherwise does not support the proposed inference.
The statements belong to different speakers, objections, hypotheses, dialectical stages, or reported positions.
The contrary statement denies only a stronger or different proposition than the one validly implied.
A missing premise has been supplied by the investigator without documentary warrant.
Translation, textual corruption, transcription, or witness variation creates the apparent chain.
The conflict is an ordinary error or unresolved inconsistency rather than a literary operation.
disqualifying_conditions: No recoverable bridge statement connects the anchor to the alleged implication.
The selected contrary statement directly denies the anchor, so the selected relation is not LC-003.
The alleged derived implication is actually pronounced in the selected path and the contradiction is direct.
Term identity or equivalence fails at any required step.
The inference rule is invalid, incomplete, circular, or dependent on an undocumented premise.
The contrary statement does not deny the derived implication after scope and attribution are reconstructed.
Source integrity, voice attribution, or proposition identity cannot be established.
The chain is supplied only by semantic similarity, graph traversal, analogy, or investigator intuition.
authorized_engine_action: Register the chain as a noncanonical development candidate. Recover and preserve every pronounced passage before constructing the derivation. Reconstruct the anchor, bridge, and contrary statements independently before comparison. Write each inference step explicitly and identify the rule that licenses it. Mark every unpronounced proposition as a derived analytical object, never as a quotation. Test term identity, scope, condition, modality, attribution, and translation at every step. Search the whole work for direct formulations, repeated bridge relations, and counterexamples. Test all ordinary alternatives and preserve unresolved ones. Evaluate LC-001, LC-002, or other techniques separately when they also apply. Open a bounded interpretive inquiry only after the implication contradiction survives adversarial testing. Terminate with underdetermination when the inference cannot be warranted documentarily.
prohibited_inferences: Do not represent a reconstructed implication as words pronounced by the author.
Do not infer deliberate concealment merely because a valid implication contradiction exists.
Do not infer which statement is true, private, unconventional, esoteric, or authorially preferred.
Do not assume transitivity, equivalence, or term identity without explicit justification.
Do not fill a missing premise from general knowledge, doctrinal expectation, or another author without a governed evidence path.
Do not treat semantic similarity, vector proximity, or graph connectivity as logical implication.
Do not import LC-018's frequency rule or any truth-selection rule into LC-003.
Do not merge LC-003 with LC-001, LC-002, or another technique merely because they co-occur.
Do not convert a candidate chain into a documented finding while ordinary alternatives remain unresolved.
uncertainty_note: Until source integrity, proposition identity, voice, term continuity, every bridge premise, the inference rule, and the contrary relation are documented and ordinary alternatives tested, the engine must preserve the chain only as a candidate implication contradiction and must not attribute the derived proposition to the author.

name: Apparent Repetition with Minute Addition or Omission
strauss_formulation: to contradict the first statement not directly, but by seemingly repeating it while actually adding to it, or omitting from it, an apparently negligible expression
mechanism: A later passage presents itself as a repetition of an earlier statement, but adds or omits a small expression. The apparent sameness lowers the reader's attention to the verbal difference, while the difference changes the proposition enough to make the later statement incompatible with the earlier one.
documentary_function: Direct bounded documentary attention under LC-004 without authorizing a concealed-teaching inference.
investigative_requirement: Establish that the passages are genuine parallels, align them word by word, record every addition and omission, reconstruct the semantic effect of each difference, and determine whether a minute difference—not some independent change of context, voice, scope, condition, or translation—produces a substantive contradiction.
distinguished_from: LC-004 requires apparent repetition plus a minute addition or omission whose documented semantic effect produces contradiction. Mere verbal variation does not trigger the technique.
LC-004 differs from LC-003 because the contradiction is carried by an altered repetition rather than by an unpronounced logical implication.
LC-004 differs from LC-005 because no intermediary assertion is required. LC-005 uses a sequence in which the contradiction creeps in through a repetition of an intermediary statement.

The evaluator may identify a bounded altered-repetition structure, but it may not infer that the smaller expression is authorially privileged, that concealment was intended, or that either statement is true.

Typography, punctuation, spelling, inflection, translation, or witness variation must not be treated as substantive without a documented effect on proposition, scope, modality, condition, attribution, or negation.
An addition and an omission must be preserved as separate textual operations even when they occur in the same parallel pair.
minimum_trigger_features: Two recoverable passages occur in the same work.
The passages have been established as genuine parallels concerning the same subject.
The later passage presents itself as a repetition or near-repetition of the earlier passage.
A word-by-word alignment reveals at least one addition or omission.
The relevant difference is small relative to the repeated verbal structure.
The semantic effect of the difference makes the normalized propositions incompatible.
required_corroboration_features: The same minute expression has a stable technical function elsewhere in the work.
The author elsewhere explicitly distinguishes the formulations.
Another parallel passage reproduces the same addition or omission pattern.
The altered wording affects negation, modality, quantification, condition, attribution, temporal scope, or subject scope.
A recoverable source or quotation confirms that the difference is deliberate at the documentary level.
A second independently reconstructed literary device converges on the same bounded textual problem.
ordinary_alternatives: Stylistic variation without propositional change.
Grammatical adjustment required by local syntax.
Abbreviation or expansion for readability.
Different speaker, addressee, genre, or argumentative function.
Different time, condition, scope, modality, or domain.
Translation choice or source-language ambiguity.
Copyist, editor, printer, or witness variation.
Quotation from a different source or version.
Ordinary imprecision, memory lapse, or compositional revision.
The passages are not genuine parallels.
disqualifying_conditions: The selected passages are not in the same work.
No genuine parallel relation can be documented.
The later passage does not present itself as a repetition or near-repetition.
No addition or omission is present after reliable alignment.
The difference is not minute relative to the shared verbal structure.
The difference has no documented propositional effect.
The normalized propositions are compatible.
The apparent difference is fully explained by witness, edition, translation, grammar, voice, scope, or context.
The contradiction depends on silently changing the meaning of a shared term.
Source integrity or passage attribution cannot be resolved.
authorized_engine_action: Preserve both passages and their complete contexts. Generate an explicit alignment record with additions and omissions marked separately. Record the semantic function of each difference without assigning hidden meaning. Normalize both propositions and document the incompatibility relation. Test every ordinary alternative and preserve unresolved explanations. Search the work for further parallel formulations and counterexamples. Evaluate LC-001, LC-002, LC-003, or LC-005 separately when their conditions are independently present. Terminate at candidate status when the semantic effect or corroboration remains insufficient.
prohibited_inferences: Do not treat every wording difference as significant.
Do not infer contradiction from lexical difference alone.
Do not infer concealment or authorial intention from apparent repetition alone.
Do not infer that the shorter, longer, earlier, or later formulation is true.
Do not infer that the minute expression carries an esoteric meaning.
Do not ignore changes of speaker, scope, condition, time, genre, or translation.
Do not merge LC-004 with LC-003 or LC-005.
Do not represent repository test fixtures as examples supplied by Strauss.
uncertainty_note: When the passages appear parallel but the semantic effect of the addition or omission remains contestable, preserve the pair as a candidate altered repetition and record all rival explanations. Do not promote it to a corroborated contradiction.

name: Intermediary Assertion and Creeping Contradiction
strauss_formulation: to introduce between the two contradictory statements an intermediary assertion
mechanism: A first statement is followed by an intermediary assertion compatible with it. A later statement appears to repeat the intermediary assertion but changes it through a minute addition or omission. The altered repetition now contradicts the first statement. The contradiction is concealed by the apparent continuity between the intermediary and final formulations.
documentary_function: Direct bounded documentary attention under LC-005 without authorizing a concealed-teaching inference.
investigative_requirement: Reconstruct the complete three-stage sequence: first statement, intermediary assertion, and final altered repetition. Establish that the intermediary does not contradict the first statement, align the intermediary and final statements word by word, record the minute addition or omission, document its semantic effect, and establish that the final proposition—not the intermediary—contradicts the first proposition.
distinguished_from: LC-005 requires three ordered stages. A two-passage altered repetition belongs to LC-004 unless a distinct intermediary assertion is documented.
The intermediary assertion must remain compatible with the first statement. If it already contradicts the first, the creeping mechanism is not established.
The final statement must present itself as a repetition or near-repetition of the intermediary, not merely as another statement on the same subject.
The minute addition or omission must have a documented semantic effect that changes the final proposition enough to contradict the first proposition.
LC-005 does not authorize the inference that the final statement is true, that the author intended concealment, or that the sequence expresses a hidden teaching.
Additions and omissions must be recorded separately, and the intermediary must not be silently reconstructed from the first or final statement.
minimum_trigger_features: Three recoverable passages occur in the same work and in documented sequence.
All three passages concern the same subject or proposition family.
The intermediary assertion is propositionally compatible with the first statement.
The final statement presents itself as a repetition or near-repetition of the intermediary.
Alignment reveals at least one minute addition or omission between intermediary and final statements.
The minute difference has a documented semantic effect.
The final statement contradicts the first statement.
The intermediary statement itself does not contradict the first statement.
required_corroboration_features: The same minute expression has a stable technical function elsewhere in the work.
Another three-stage sequence reproduces the same creeping pattern.
The author elsewhere explicitly distinguishes the intermediary and final formulations.
The altered wording affects negation, modality, quantification, condition, attribution, or scope.
A recoverable source confirms that the intermediary-final difference is documentarily significant.
A separately reconstructed literary device converges on the same bounded sequence.
ordinary_alternatives: Ordinary development or revision of thought.
A change in speaker, audience, genre, or argumentative role.
A shift in temporal, conditional, modal, or subject scope.
Abbreviation or expansion for readability.
Grammatical adjustment required by local syntax.
Translation or source-language variation.
Copyist, editor, printer, or witness variation.
Quotation from different sources or versions.
The intermediary and final passages are not genuine parallels.
The intermediary already contradicts the first statement.
The final statement does not contradict the first statement.
Ordinary imprecision, memory lapse, or compositional inconsistency.
disqualifying_conditions: Fewer than three distinct passages are recoverable.
The passage order is not established.
The passages are not in the same work.
The intermediary assertion contradicts the first statement.
The intermediary and final passages are not genuine parallels.
The final passage does not present itself as a repetition or near-repetition of the intermediary.
No addition or omission remains after reliable alignment.
The difference has no documented propositional effect.
The final proposition is compatible with the first proposition.
The contradiction depends on silently changing the meaning of a shared term.
The pattern is fully explained by context, voice, scope, translation, witness, or ordinary revision.
Source integrity or passage attribution remains unresolved.
authorized_engine_action: Preserve the three passages in sequence with complete local contexts. Generate a sequence record distinguishing first, intermediary, and final stages. Align intermediary and final passages and mark additions and omissions separately. Record the semantic effect of each difference without assigning hidden meaning. Normalize all three propositions and document compatibility and incompatibility relations. Test every ordinary alternative and preserve unresolved explanations. Search the work for additional intermediary sequences and counterexamples. Evaluate LC-004 separately for the intermediary-final pair when warranted. Terminate at candidate status when the intermediary role, semantic effect, or corroboration remains insufficient.
prohibited_inferences: Do not infer a creeping contradiction from any three passages merely because they occur in sequence.
Do not treat the intermediary as contradictory when it is only preparatory or ambiguous.
Do not infer contradiction from wording difference alone.
Do not infer concealment or intention from the sequence alone.
Do not infer that the final statement is truer, deeper, or authorially preferred.
Do not collapse LC-005 into LC-004.
Do not ignore changes of speaker, scope, condition, time, genre, translation, or witness.
Do not represent test fixtures as examples supplied by Strauss.
uncertainty_note: When the intermediary role, semantic effect, or proposition relations remain contestable, preserve the sequence as a candidate and record competing explanations. Do not promote the sequence to a corroborated creeping contradiction.

name: Use of Ambiguous Words
strauss_formulation: To use ambiguous words.
mechanism: A single word or compact expression sustains more than one contextually available meaning. One meaning may support the exterior or conventional reading, while another supports a distinct interior or concealed reading. The verbal surface remains identical even though the proposition changes with the selected sense.
documentary_function: Direct bounded documentary attention under LC-006 without authorizing a concealed-teaching inference.
investigative_requirement: Identify the exact lexical item, reconstruct each historically and contextually available sense, preserve source-language evidence, substitute each sense into the passage without changing the surrounding syntax, and test whether the resulting propositions are genuinely distinct and both locally viable. Ambiguity must be demonstrated, not merely imagined.
distinguished_from: LC-006 concerns semantic ambiguity in a word or expression. It is not triggered merely because a sentence is vague, difficult, metaphorical, elliptical, or badly translated.

LC-007 concerns the broader two-faced communicative structure of a speech addressed differently to different audiences. LC-006 may operate within LC-007 but does not by itself establish audience differentiation.

LC-008 concerns concealment concentrated in a single common word because of its small and unobtrusive placement. LC-006 requires multiple senses, whereas LC-008 may concern an ordinary word whose significance is hidden even when it is not semantically ambiguous.
LC-009 concerns a recurrent secret terminology requiring corpus-level indexing. A single ambiguous occurrence does not establish a secret technical vocabulary.
Dictionary polysemy alone is insufficient. The competing senses must be documentarily recoverable and syntactically viable in the specific passage.

The evaluator may establish a bounded ambiguity structure but may not determine that one sense is the hidden truth, that the author intended concealment, or that different audiences actually received different meanings.
minimum_trigger_features: A recoverable word or compact expression is identified in a stable passage.
At least two distinct senses are documentarily attested or contextually recoverable.
Each proposed sense fits the same local syntax without silently rewriting the sentence.
Each sense yields a proposition materially different from the other.
The local context does not conclusively eliminate one sense.
required_corroboration_features: The same word demonstrably bears both senses elsewhere in the work.
The author elsewhere distinguishes or exploits the two senses.
Parallel passages support different readings of the same lexical item.
The source language preserves an ambiguity obscured by translation.
A contradiction or structural difficulty disappears under one sense but remains under the other.
A separate literary device independently draws attention to the same word.
ordinary_alternatives: Ordinary dictionary polysemy with one contextually dominant meaning.
Translation ambiguity not present in the source language.
Metaphor, analogy, idiom, or technical usage rather than ambiguity.
Vagueness or underspecification.
Grammatical or syntactic uncertainty.
Textual corruption or witness variation.
A change in speaker, genre, quotation source, or argumentative level.
Reader ignorance of a standard technical term.
Ordinary imprecision or inconsistent usage.
disqualifying_conditions: Only one sense is documentarily attested in the relevant linguistic horizon.
One proposed sense cannot fit the local grammar or syntax.
The competing senses produce materially equivalent propositions.
Local context decisively excludes one sense.
The ambiguity exists only in translation and not in the source language.
The reading depends on importing a later or anachronistic meaning.
The lexical item is reconstructed incorrectly or the witness is unstable.
The result depends on changing surrounding words, punctuation, or syntax without evidence.
The passage is merely vague, obscure, or metaphorical rather than semantically ambiguous.
authorized_engine_action: Preserve the passage and exact lexical item. Create separate sense records with documentary support for each. Generate a syntactically faithful rendering and proposition for each sense. Test local context, source language, translation, and ordinary disambiguation. Search the work for every occurrence of the lexical item and its variants. Record which senses remain viable and which are eliminated. Evaluate LC-007, LC-008, or LC-009 separately when their independent conditions are present. Terminate at candidate status when the senses or their viability remain uncertain.
prohibited_inferences: Do not infer hidden meaning from dictionary polysemy alone.
Do not invent unattested senses.
Do not privilege the rarer, technical, or unconventional sense automatically.
Do not infer audience differentiation from ambiguity alone.
Do not infer secret terminology from a single occurrence.
Do not treat translation ambiguity as authorial ambiguity without source-language support.
Do not infer concealment, intention, or truth selection from the existence of two viable senses.
Do not collapse LC-006 into LC-007, LC-008, or LC-009.
uncertainty_note: When two senses are plausible but their documentary attestation, syntactic viability, or contextual survival is incomplete, preserve a candidate ambiguity with explicit rival sense records. Do not select an interior meaning or concealed teaching.

name: Two-Faced Speech
strauss_formulation: A speech may have two faces: an exterior face useful for social or political purposes and an inner face useful for knowledge of the truth.
mechanism: The same verbal surface supports two documentarily distinguishable readings with different communicative functions. One reading is accessible and useful within an ordinary social or political horizon. Another reading is available to a more independently prepared reader and bears a distinct knowledge-oriented function. The technique concerns differentiated communication, not merely lexical ambiguity.
documentary_function: Direct bounded documentary attention under LC-007 without authorizing a concealed-teaching inference.
investigative_requirement: Reconstruct the same textual unit under two independently supported readings; document the audience horizon and function of each reading; establish that both readings arise from the same verbal surface without rewriting it; test whether ordinary genre, pedagogy, irony, ambiguity, or contextual variation sufficiently explains the difference; and preserve the possibility that the apparent duality is only a repository hypothesis.
distinguished_from: LC-007 concerns a whole communicative structure with differentiated readings, functions, and audience horizons. LC-006 concerns multiple senses of a word or compact expression.
LC-007 may employ LC-006, but lexical ambiguity is neither necessary nor sufficient for two-faced speech.
LC-008 concerns concealment concentrated in a small common word. LC-007 concerns the relation between an exterior and an interior face of a speech.
LC-009 concerns recurrent secret terminology. A two-faced speech may use ordinary vocabulary and need not establish a technical lexicon.
A difference between novice and expert comprehension does not by itself establish two-faced speech; the two readings must have distinct documentary support and communicative functions.

The evaluator may identify a bounded dual-reading structure but may not declare the interior reading true, infer persecution, infer deliberate concealment, or assign actual readers to audience classes without evidence.
minimum_trigger_features: A bounded speech, passage, chapter, or textual unit is recoverable in a stable witness.
The same verbal surface supports at least two materially distinct readings.
One reading has a documented ordinary, public, social, pedagogical, or political function.
A second reading has a documented knowledge-oriented, philosophical, or independently reconstructive function.
The readings can be associated with distinct audience horizons on documentary grounds.
Neither reading requires silently altering the text.
The local context does not conclusively collapse the two readings into one.
required_corroboration_features: The author explicitly distinguishes audiences or levels of readers.
The work elsewhere repeats the same public/private or ordinary/independent distinction.
A preface, address to the reader, or structural marker differentiates communicative levels.
The exterior reading coheres with a documented social or political function.
The interior reading is independently supported by contradictions, source recovery, architecture, or another bounded literary device.
Different passages explicitly preserve both readings without resolving them into one.
A documented reception history shows distinct readings among differently prepared readers.
ordinary_alternatives: Ordinary pedagogical simplification.
Difference between introductory and advanced exposition.
Genre convention or rhetorical amplification.
Lexical ambiguity adequately handled under LC-006.
Irony, metaphor, or analogy without differentiated audience structure.
Change in speaker, narrator, quotation source, or dramatic role.
Different historical contexts or stages of composition.
Translation or textual-variant effects.
Reader disagreement not intended or structured by the text.
Ordinary inconsistency, vagueness, or imprecision.
disqualifying_conditions: Only one materially supported reading remains.
The proposed readings require changing the verbal surface.
One reading lacks textual or documentary support.
The audience horizons are invented rather than documented.
The functions of the two readings are materially the same.
The duality is fully explained by ordinary pedagogy, genre, speaker change, or translation.
The result depends solely on a single ambiguous word without broader communicative evidence.
The interior reading is asserted only because it appears more philosophical or unconventional.
The witness, attribution, or textual boundary is unstable.
The text itself decisively resolves the apparent duality.
authorized_engine_action: Preserve the bounded textual unit and its full context. Create separate exterior and interior reading records. Link each reading to exact textual support, proposition, function, and audience horizon. Document the shared verbal surface and the evidence distinguishing the two readings. Test ordinary pedagogical, generic, rhetorical, lexical, dramatic, and textual alternatives. Search the work for explicit audience distinctions and parallel dual-reading structures. Evaluate LC-006, LC-008, LC-009, LC-011, or LC-012 separately when independently triggered. Preserve rival explanations and terminate at candidate status when audience or function evidence remains incomplete.
prohibited_inferences: Do not infer two-faced speech from difficulty alone.
Do not infer audience differentiation from lexical ambiguity alone.
Do not declare the interior reading true or the exterior reading false.
Do not infer persecution or danger unless separately documented.
Do not infer deliberate concealment from the existence of two readings.
Do not assume that sophisticated readers necessarily receive the interior reading.
Do not treat ordinary pedagogy as esotericism without further evidence.
Do not collapse LC-007 into LC-006, LC-008, LC-009, LC-011, or LC-012.
uncertainty_note: When two readings remain plausible but audience differentiation, communicative function, or the relation between the readings is incomplete, preserve a candidate two-faced structure with rival explanations. Do not label either reading the true or concealed teaching.

name: Concealment in a Single Common Word
strauss_formulation: A secret is more perfectly concealed in a word than in a sentence because the word is smaller and therefore a better hiding place; common words placed unobtrusively within unobtrusive sentences are especially effective.
mechanism: A word that appears ordinary and unremarkable occupies a small, low-prominence position within an equally unobtrusive sentence. Independent documentary analysis shows that the word has a materially significant role in the proposition, argument, or doctrinal relation. Its common appearance prevents it from attracting the attention that a conspicuous technical expression would receive.
documentary_function: Direct bounded documentary attention under LC-008 without authorizing a concealed-teaching inference.
investigative_requirement: Do not scan every common word for secrets. Begin only when an independently documented textual difficulty, source relation, repetition, contradiction, architectural feature, or explicit authorial indication draws attention to the word. Establish its ordinary surface character, its low-prominence placement, its exact semantic and propositional effect, and the documentary basis for treating it as significant.
distinguished_from: LC-008 concerns the smallness, commonness, and unobtrusive placement of a word. The word need not be semantically ambiguous.
LC-006 requires two or more viable senses of a word or expression. LC-008 may be triggered by one ordinary word whose significance lies in placement or doctrinal effect.
LC-009 requires recurrent secret terminology and corpus-level indexing. One common word in one passage does not establish a secret vocabulary.
LC-002 concerns an incidental contradictory statement. LC-008 concerns a word-level carrier within an unobtrusive sentence.
LC-020 concerns hints generally. A common word may function as a hint, but LC-008 requires the specific word-level and low-prominence structure.
The evaluator may identify a corroborated common-word signal but may not infer a secret, hidden teaching, authorial intention, or doctrinal truth.
minimum_trigger_features: A precise word is recoverable in a stable passage.
The word is ordinary or common within the relevant linguistic and historical horizon.
The word is placed in a low-prominence position within an unobtrusive sentence.
An independent documentary trigger directs attention to the word.
The word has a documented semantic, propositional, structural, or doctrinal effect.
Removing, replacing, or differently construing the word materially changes the relevant relation.
required_corroboration_features: The same word has a stable significant function elsewhere in the work.
The word recurs at architectonically important locations.
A source comparison shows that the word was deliberately retained, added, or altered at the documentary level.
A contradiction, implication chain, omission, repetition, or structural pattern independently converges on the word.
The author elsewhere explicitly comments on the word or its semantic field.
The sentence's apparent incidentalness contrasts with the word's measurable effect on the proposition.
ordinary_alternatives: The word is ordinary and insignificant.
The effect is a translation artifact.
The word is required only by grammar or idiom.
The sentence is concise for stylistic reasons.
The apparent significance is produced by punctuation or editorial intervention.
The word's effect disappears in the source language.
The word belongs to a quotation or another speaker.
The word is merely emphatic, connective, or formulaic.
The interpretation depends on anachronistic semantics.
The word was introduced by copying, printing, or witness variation.
disqualifying_conditions: No independent documentary trigger justifies attention to the word.
The word is not securely recoverable in the witness.
The word is conspicuous or explicitly technical rather than common and unobtrusive.
The sentence is already a prominent doctrinal declaration.
Removing or replacing the word produces no material change.
The word's significance exists only in translation.
Grammar, idiom, quotation, punctuation, or witness variation fully explains the effect.
The claimed significance requires inventing an unattested meaning.
The local or architectonic context decisively excludes the proposed role.
The result rests solely on the general instruction to scrutinize every word.
authorized_engine_action: Preserve the exact word, sentence, witness, and locations. Record the independent documentary trigger that directed attention to the word. Document commonness, sentence prominence, and word placement separately. Generate source-language, translation, and counterfactual renderings. Record the word's exact effect on proposition, scope, relation, or architecture. Search the work for every occurrence and positional pattern of the word. Test grammatical, stylistic, translational, editorial, and witness alternatives. Evaluate LC-006, LC-009, LC-002, or LC-020 separately when independently triggered. Terminate at candidate status when significance or corroboration remains incomplete.
prohibited_inferences: Do not search every common word indiscriminately for hidden meaning.
Do not infer significance from commonness or smallness alone.
Do not infer concealment from low prominence alone.
Do not invent a technical or esoteric meaning.
Do not treat translation artifacts as authorial devices.
Do not infer that the word states the author's true teaching.
Do not infer authorial intention or a secret audience.
Do not collapse LC-008 into LC-006, LC-009, LC-002, or LC-020.
uncertainty_note: When the word is common and unobtrusive but its material effect or independent documentary warrant remains contestable, preserve a candidate common-word signal with rival explanations. Do not infer a hidden meaning.

name: Secret Terminology
strauss_formulation: We may call those words secret words. His secret terminology requires a special study.
mechanism: A recurring vocabulary carries stable or patterned meanings that are not exhausted by ordinary surface usage. Some terms may be ambiguous; others may be unambiguous yet function as coded or technical markers. The terminology operates across multiple passages, so no single occurrence is sufficient to establish the system.
documentary_function: Direct bounded documentary attention under LC-009 without authorizing a concealed-teaching inference.
investigative_requirement: Construct a corpus-level occurrence index for each candidate term; preserve exact forms, variants, translations, speakers, locations, and contexts; classify ordinary, technical, ambiguous, and contextually coded uses; test whether a stable semantic or functional pattern survives ordinary lexical, generic, translational, and contextual explanations; and refuse to designate a secret term from frequency alone.
distinguished_from: LC-009 is corpus-level. A single occurrence cannot establish secret terminology.
LC-006 concerns lexical ambiguity in a bounded passage. LC-009 may include ambiguous terms but also permits unambiguous terms whose coded function appears only across uses.
LC-008 concerns an unobtrusive common word in a bounded passage. LC-009 requires recurrent patterning across a work or authorized corpus.
Repetition alone is insufficient. The occurrences must exhibit a stable semantic, functional, audience, positional, or doctrinal pattern.
A technical term is not automatically a secret term. Ordinary disciplinary vocabulary must be tested as an alternative.
The evaluator may identify a corroborated terminology pattern but may not declare a hidden teaching, identify the true doctrine, or infer concealment or intention.
minimum_trigger_features: A candidate lexical item or phrase occurs in multiple recoverable passages.
The occurrence set is sufficiently complete for the declared scope.
Occurrences are normalized across inflectional, orthographic, and translational variants.
At least two context classes or usage functions are represented.
A stable semantic, functional, positional, audience, or doctrinal pattern is documented.
The pattern is not explained by frequency alone.
required_corroboration_features: The term repeatedly changes function at consistent architectonic locations.
The author explicitly distinguishes the term or its users.
The term clusters with other documented literary devices.
A source comparison shows patterned alteration, retention, or substitution.
The term has a stable restricted meaning in otherwise diverse contexts.
A related term exhibits a complementary distribution.
The terminology pattern predicts independently verified passage distinctions.
ordinary_alternatives: Ordinary technical vocabulary.
Genre-specific or disciplinary usage.
Translation convention.
Terminological inconsistency.
Semantic drift across contexts.
Different speakers or quoted authorities.
Editorial normalization.
High frequency caused by subject matter.
Stylistic preference.
Reader overclassification of loosely related uses.
The occurrence index is incomplete or biased.
disqualifying_conditions: The candidate appears only once within the declared scope.
The occurrence index is materially incomplete without explicit limitation.
Occurrences are not normalized across textual variants and translations.
No stable pattern survives contextual reconstruction.
The pattern is explained fully by ordinary technical usage or subject matter.
The proposed coded function depends on inventing unattested meanings.
Different speakers or sources account for the variation.
Counterexamples equal or exceed supporting cases without a principled distinction.
The pattern disappears in the source language.
The designation rests only on frequency, rarity, or thematic importance.
authorized_engine_action: Create a complete occurrence index within the declared scope. Preserve each occurrence as an independent record. Normalize variants without deleting the original forms. Classify ordinary, technical, ambiguous, and candidate-coded uses separately. Map distributions by speaker, section, audience, source, and doctrinal context. Test ordinary technical, generic, translational, editorial, and thematic alternatives. Search for negative cases and disconfirming distributions. Evaluate LC-006 and LC-008 separately for individual occurrences when warranted. Terminate at candidate status when corpus coverage, pattern stability, or corroboration is incomplete.
prohibited_inferences: Do not designate a secret term from one occurrence.
Do not infer secret terminology from repetition alone.
Do not treat every technical term as a secret word.
Do not ignore negative cases.
Do not collapse distinct lexical items into one family without documentary basis.
Do not infer a hidden meaning merely because a term has multiple senses.
Do not infer concealment, authorial intention, audience, or doctrinal truth.
Do not collapse LC-009 into LC-006 or LC-008.
uncertainty_note: When a recurring term exhibits a suggestive distribution but corpus coverage, usage classification, or ordinary alternatives remain incomplete, preserve a candidate terminology pattern with explicit negative cases and unresolved uses.

name: Intentional Sophism
strauss_formulation: Strauss refers to "intentional sophisms" as devices used by Maimonides, though he does not elaborate them in this immediate passage.
mechanism: A bounded argument appears to support a conclusion but contains a documented logical, semantic, evidentiary, or inferential defect that materially shapes the reader's apparent conclusion. The current source passage does not authorize a subtype taxonomy or permit intention to be inferred from the defect alone.
documentary_function: Direct bounded documentary attention under LC-010 without authorizing a concealed-teaching inference.
investigative_requirement: Reconstruct the argument exactly; distinguish premises, inference steps, and conclusion; identify the alleged defect under a stated logical or evidentiary standard; test whether the defect materially affects the conclusion; investigate ordinary error, compression, pedagogy, translation, corruption, and speaker alternatives; and keep sophistic structure separate from intentional deployment.
minimum_trigger_features: A bounded argument is recoverable in a stable witness.
Premises, inference steps, and conclusion can be reconstructed without silently repairing the argument.
A specific inferential, logical, semantic, or evidentiary defect is documented under an explicit standard.
The alleged defect materially affects the apparent support for the conclusion.
The defect is not merely disagreement with the conclusion.
required_corroboration_features: The same defect pattern recurs under comparable conditions elsewhere in the work.
The author elsewhere supplies the missing distinction or correction.
A source comparison shows a controlled alteration that creates the defective inference.
Another independently reconstructed literary device converges on the same argument.
The author demonstrates competence with the governing distinction elsewhere.
The defective argument produces a predictable surface conclusion contradicted by stronger documentary evidence elsewhere.
ordinary_alternatives: Unintentional logical error.
Compressed argument with an unstated but recoverable premise.
Pedagogical simplification.
Rhetorical abbreviation.
Translation error or source-language ambiguity.
Textual corruption, punctuation, or witness variation.
Different speaker, objection, quotation, or hypothetical.
Change of domain, scope, modality, or definition.
Ordinary inconsistency or compositional revision.
The investigator has reconstructed the argument incorrectly.
The conclusion remains independently supported despite the alleged defect.
disqualifying_conditions: No bounded argument can be reconstructed.
The alleged defect is only disagreement with the conclusion.
The defect disappears when explicit premises or context are restored.
Correcting the alleged defect does not materially change the argument's support.
The passage belongs to another speaker, quotation, objection, or hypothetical not yet resolved.
Translation, textual corruption, punctuation, or witness variation fully explains the problem.
The analysis silently introduces or removes a premise.
The defect depends on an anachronistic logical or semantic standard.
The argument is merely incomplete rather than misleading or fallacious.
Source integrity or argument attribution remains unresolved.
authorized_engine_action: Preserve the complete argument and witness. Create separate premise, inference-step, and conclusion records. Mark explicit and reconstructed premises distinctly. Document the alleged defect and governing standard. Generate a counterfactual corrected argument without overwriting the original. Test all ordinary error, compression, pedagogical, rhetorical, textual, and attribution alternatives. Search the work for corrections, repetitions, parallel arguments, and counterexamples. Evaluate LC-003, LC-004, LC-005, LC-006, LC-011, LC-014, or LC-015 separately when independently triggered. Preserve intentionality as unresolved unless direct documentary evidence is separately established.
prohibited_inferences: Do not infer sophism merely because an argument is false or unpersuasive.
Do not infer intention from the existence of a defect.
Do not infer concealment from a fallacious structure alone.
Do not create a canonical subtype taxonomy from the present source passage.
Do not silently repair the argument before evaluating it.
Do not treat reconstructed premises as direct quotations.
Do not infer that the defective conclusion is the author's own position.
Do not select the hidden or true teaching.
Do not collapse LC-010 into adjacent techniques.
uncertainty_note: When an argument appears defective but reconstruction, governing standard, material effect, attribution, or ordinary alternatives remain contestable, preserve a candidate sophistic structure. Intentionality remains unresolved.

name: Ironical Remark
strauss_formulation: Strauss names "ironical remarks" among Maimonides' devices without elaborating their varieties here.
mechanism: A recoverable remark has a viable surface proposition, yet contextual, dramatic, argumentative, lexical, or architectonic evidence supports a materially different nonliteral proposition. The divergence may involve reversal, qualification, exposure, or distancing; it must not be reduced automatically to saying the exact opposite.
documentary_function: Direct bounded documentary attention under LC-011 without authorizing a concealed-teaching inference.
investigative_requirement: Preserve the literal wording; reconstruct the surface proposition before proposing a nonliteral reading; identify the speaker, target, object, and context; record each textual marker supporting divergence; test whether quotation, humor, sarcasm, parody, hyperbole, understatement, ambiguity, dramatic characterization, translation, or ordinary inconsistency better explains the passage; and never convert contextual tension into proof of authorial intention.
minimum_trigger_features: A bounded remark is recoverable in a stable witness.
A literal surface proposition can be reconstructed.
A materially distinct candidate nonliteral proposition can be reconstructed without changing the words.
At least one documentary marker supports divergence between surface and candidate nonliteral propositions.
The proposed divergence is not merely disagreement with the surface proposition.
required_corroboration_features: The speaker elsewhere explicitly rejects the surface proposition.
The remark conflicts with a stable position documented elsewhere while the nonliteral reading resolves the conflict.
A repeated ironic pattern occurs under comparable dramatic or argumentative conditions.
The wording contains controlled exaggeration, praise, understatement, or incongruity independently supported by context.
The target responds as though the remark has a nonliteral force.
Architectonic placement or another independently reconstructed literary device converges on the same divergence.
A source comparison shows deliberate retention or alteration that intensifies the nonliteral effect.
ordinary_alternatives: Literal statement.
Sarcasm or ridicule without broader doctrinal significance.
Humor, wit, parody, or comic exaggeration.
Hyperbole or understatement.
Metaphor, analogy, idiom, or ambiguity.
Quotation, objection, hypothetical, or reported opinion.
Dramatic characterization of a speaker.
Change of audience, scope, time, condition, or definition.
Translation, punctuation, or textual-variant effect.
Ordinary inconsistency, imprecision, or compositional revision.
The investigator has projected tone not supported by the text.
disqualifying_conditions: No stable remark or textual boundary can be recovered.
The surface proposition has not been reconstructed.
The candidate nonliteral proposition requires changing or adding words without evidence.
The two propositions are materially equivalent.
No documentary marker supports nonliteral divergence.
Literal coherence remains complete and no counterevidence exists.
Quotation, speaker attribution, genre, humor, hyperbole, ambiguity, translation, or witness variation fully explains the effect.
The analysis depends solely on perceived tone.
The nonliteral reading is preferred only because it is more unconventional or philosophically attractive.
The passage is attributed to the author without resolving dramatic or quoted voice.
authorized_engine_action: Preserve the exact remark and its full context. Create separate surface and candidate nonliteral proposition records. Record the divergence relation without collapsing it into exact negation. Document speaker, target, object, audience, and dramatic role separately. Index every marker and countermarker. Test literal coherence and all ordinary rhetorical, dramatic, lexical, textual, and compositional alternatives. Search the work for parallel remarks, explicit corrections, reactions, and counterexamples. Evaluate LC-006, LC-007, LC-010, LC-012, LC-020, or LC-021 separately when independently triggered. Preserve intention and intended meaning as unresolved.
prohibited_inferences: Do not infer irony from disagreement with the literal statement.
Do not infer irony from tone alone.
Do not assume irony always means the exact opposite.
Do not treat humor, sarcasm, hyperbole, or ambiguity automatically as irony.
Do not attribute a character's irony directly to the author.
Do not infer the intended meaning or intended audience.
Do not infer concealment, hidden teaching, ridicule, or doctrinal truth.
Do not create canonical irony subtypes from the present source.
Do not collapse LC-011 into adjacent techniques.
uncertainty_note: When a material surface/nonliteral divergence is plausible but the speaker, target, markers, literal coherence, or ordinary alternatives remain contested, preserve a candidate ironic divergence with both proposition records and all rival explanations. Do not identify the intended meaning.

name: Apostrophe to the Reader
strauss_formulation: "various kinds of apostrophes to the reader"
mechanism: The ordinary exposition is interrupted by a direct address to a reader or reader class. The address may change the communicative relation by warning, inviting, restricting, commanding attention, withholding, permitting, or differentiating levels of readership. Its evidentiary force depends on exact form, addressee, placement, and local function.
documentary_function: Direct bounded documentary attention under LC-012 without authorizing a concealed-teaching inference.
investigative_requirement: Recover the exact address; establish that its grammatical and dramatic addressee is the reader rather than a character, deity, source, or interlocutor; document its position within the argument and work; reconstruct its immediate communicative function; compare all other reader addresses in the declared scope; and test whether genre, pedagogy, rhetoric, translation, or ordinary author-reader convention fully explains it.
minimum_trigger_features: A bounded direct-address expression is recoverable in a stable witness.
The grammatical or rhetorical addressee is documentarily identified as the reader or a reader class.
The address interrupts, frames, redirects, qualifies, or otherwise modifies the surrounding exposition.
Its form or placement has a documented communicative effect.
The effect is not exhausted by the mere presence of second-person grammar.
required_corroboration_features: The work contains repeated reader addresses with systematically different forms or placements.
The author explicitly distinguishes types or levels of readers elsewhere.
The address occurs immediately before or after a restriction, warning, omission, disclosure, or invitation.
A later passage answers, revises, or limits the same reader address.
The address appears at an architectonically marked location.
A separately reconstructed device converges on the same audience or disclosure problem.
A source comparison shows deliberate addition, retention, or repositioning of the address.
ordinary_alternatives: Generic rhetorical address.
Ordinary pedagogical encouragement.
Conventional preface or sermon formula.
Apostrophe to a deity, character, city, law, muse, or absent person rather than the reader.
Second-person grammar directed to a dramatic interlocutor.
Quotation from another source.
Translation convention not present in the source language.
Stylistic variation or emotional emphasis.
Editorial heading, punctuation, or interpolation.
Broad inclusive language without reader differentiation.
disqualifying_conditions: The addressee cannot be resolved as the reader.
The address is supplied only by a translator, editor, or heading.
The expression is part of a quotation or dramatic exchange not attributable to the authorial address.
The surrounding exposition is not modified in any documentable way.
The claimed function rests only on second-person grammar.
Genre convention fully explains the address without residual significance.
The proposed audience class is invented rather than textually supported.
Placement has been misidentified because the textual boundary or structure is unstable.
Translation, punctuation, or witness variation fully explains the form.
The analysis assumes hidden instruction before reconstructing the ordinary function.
authorized_engine_action: Preserve the exact address and complete local context. Create separate addressee, placement, and communicative-function records. Mark the function label as local and noncanonical. Index all other reader addresses within the declared scope. Compare grammatical form, placement, audience language, and surrounding restrictions or invitations. Test generic rhetorical, pedagogical, generic, dramatic, translational, and editorial alternatives. Evaluate LC-007, LC-011, LC-013, LC-014, LC-020, or LC-021 separately when independently triggered. Preserve intended audience and concealed instruction as unresolved.
prohibited_inferences: Do not infer a special audience from second-person grammar alone.
Do not infer concealment from direct address alone.
Do not assume every apostrophe is addressed to the reader.
Do not infer that the attentive, wise, or prepared reader is the intended recipient without evidence.
Do not convert local descriptive functions into canonical subtypes.
Do not infer hidden instruction, authorial intention, or doctrinal truth.
Do not collapse LC-012 into LC-007 or adjacent techniques.
Do not ignore ordinary genre and pedagogical conventions.
uncertainty_note: When a direct address is recoverable but its addressee, function, placement, or relation to differentiated readership remains contestable, preserve a candidate reader-address signal with rival explanations. Do not identify an intended reader.

name: Mottoes
strauss_formulation: "mottoes prefixed to the whole work or to individual parts"
mechanism: A brief text placed before an entire work or a bounded internal division stands outside the ordinary exposition yet bears a recoverable relation to what follows. Through quotation, allusion, compression, contrast, or source association, it may orient the reader toward a problem, distinction, sequence, or interpretive horizon that the following unit does not state directly.
documentary_function: Direct bounded documentary attention under LC-013 without authorizing a concealed-teaching inference.
investigative_requirement: Establish that the prefixed text belongs to the authorially relevant witness and is not an editorial addition; recover its source and original context where possible; define exactly which work or part it governs; reconstruct the ordinary surface function before proposing an allusive relation; map the motto's words, source context, and placement to specific features of the governed unit; and test decorative, thematic, devotional, generic, publisher-supplied, and conventional alternatives.
minimum_trigger_features: A brief prefixed text is recoverable in a stable witness.
The text precedes and is documentarily attached to a whole work or a bounded internal part.
Its governed scope is identified.
Its quotation, allusion, or source relation is recoverable or explicitly preserved as unresolved.
At least one specific documentary relation connects the motto to the governed unit.
The proposed relation exceeds mere decoration, attribution, or generic thematic resemblance.
required_corroboration_features: The governed unit repeats distinctive words or images from the motto.
The unit's structure follows a sequence implied by the motto or its source context.
A contradiction or difficulty in the unit is clarified by the motto's source context.
The author elsewhere cites or explains the same source in a compatible way.
Multiple mottoes in the work display a systematic relation to their governed parts.
The motto appears at an architectonically marked boundary.
A source comparison shows deliberate selection, truncation, alteration, or repositioning of the quoted text.
Another independently reconstructed literary device converges on the same bounded interpretive problem.
ordinary_alternatives: Decorative epigraph.
General thematic summary.
Devotional or ceremonial formula.
Conventional scriptural invocation.
Dedication or honorific attribution.
Genre convention.
Publisher, editor, translator, or later-hand addition.
A source acknowledgment without interpretive force.
A memorable phrase chosen for style.
A broad thematic resemblance produced by common vocabulary.
The investigator has overextended the motto's governed scope.
disqualifying_conditions: The prefixed text is absent from the authorially relevant witness.
The text was supplied only by an editor, publisher, translator, or later hand and is not authorially adopted.
The governed scope cannot be established.
The text is embedded within ordinary exposition rather than prefixed to a work or part.
No specific relation to the governed unit is documented.
The relation depends only on broad thematic similarity.
The proposed source attribution is false or unsupported.
The source context contradicts the proposed relation without a principled account.
Decoration, dedication, invocation, or genre convention fully explains the placement.
The analysis assumes an interpretive key before reconstructing the motto's ordinary function.
authorized_engine_action: Preserve the motto, witness, placement, and governed scope. Create separate source-text, source-context, surface-function, and allusive-relation records. Record editorial or authorial status explicitly. Map exact lexical, imagistic, structural, and source-context correspondences. Search the governed unit for repetitions, contrasts, sequences, and counterexamples. Compare all other mottoes within the declared scope. Test decorative, thematic, devotional, generic, editorial, translational, and attribution alternatives. Evaluate LC-015, LC-016, LC-017, LC-020, or LC-021 separately when independently triggered. Preserve intended meaning and concealed instruction as unresolved.
prohibited_inferences: Do not infer an interpretive key from prefatory placement alone.
Do not treat every epigraph or prefatory quotation as concealment.
Do not ignore editorial or publisher provenance.
Do not detach the motto from its original source context.
Do not extend the motto's scope beyond the documented division.
Do not infer hidden teaching, authorial intention, intended audience, or doctrinal truth.
Do not assume that the motto simply summarizes what follows.
Do not collapse LC-013 into citation, repetition, chapter heading, or hint without separate evaluation.
uncertainty_note: When a prefixed text is authentic and its governed scope is known but its source, ordinary function, or allusive relation remains contestable, preserve a candidate motto relation with competing source and scope explanations. Do not designate a hidden interpretive key.

name: Silence or Significant Omission
strauss_formulation: Another device consists in silence, i.e., the omission of something which only the learned, or the learned who are able to understand of themselves, would miss.
mechanism: A passage, discussion, list, quotation, doctrinal treatment, or structural unit omits an item that a documentarily grounded expectation makes relevant. The absence becomes investigable only because a qualified reader can reconstruct what the textual or doctrinal situation would ordinarily require. The omission alters the apparent completeness, status, scope, or force of the surrounding passage.
documentary_function: Direct bounded documentary attention under LC-014 without authorizing a concealed-teaching inference.
investigative_requirement: Construct the expectation baseline before treating absence as evidence. Recover the relevant source, parallel passage, doctrinal framework, promised scope, conventional list, or authorial cross-reference; state exactly what was expected and why; verify that the item is truly absent from the relevant witness and textual boundary; document the material effect of the omission; and test abbreviation, scope, genre, damaged text, translation, source variation, and ordinary incompleteness.
distinguished_from: Absence alone is not evidence. LC-014 requires an independently documented expectation baseline.
LC-015 is a retained subtype involving omission from a quotation. LC-014 remains the broader class and must not absorb the quotation-specific documentary operation.
LC-004 concerns a minute addition or omission between apparent repetitions. LC-014 concerns missing expected content in a larger passage, discussion, quotation, or structure.
LC-023, if later admitted, concerns omission of a qualifying relative clause at the sentential level. It must remain distinct from this broader significant-omission mechanism.
A topic may be absent because it lies outside the passage's actual scope. Scope must be reconstructed before significance is considered.
The evaluator may corroborate a significant omission but may not supply the missing teaching, infer authorial intention, identify the intended reader, or declare the surrounding passage exoteric.
minimum_trigger_features: A bounded passage, discussion, list, quotation, or structural unit is recoverable in a stable witness.
A specific expected item is identified.
The expectation is supported by an independent documentary baseline.
The expected item is absent from the relevant textual boundary.
The absence materially affects completeness, status, scope, attribution, or doctrinal force.
The proposed omission is not merely a topic the investigator wishes the author had discussed.
required_corroboration_features: A recovered source contains the omitted item.
A parallel passage in the same work includes the expected item.
The author explicitly promises, enumerates, or defines a scope that normally includes the missing item.
The omission recurs under comparable conditions elsewhere.
The missing item is supplied indirectly through another independently reconstructed device.
The omission changes the status of a claim, such as suppressing that it is common opinion.
The author elsewhere demonstrates knowledge of the omitted item.
A witness or source comparison shows controlled deletion, truncation, or noninclusion.
ordinary_alternatives: The item lies outside the actual scope.
Ordinary abbreviation or summary.
Genre convention.
Pedagogical sequencing.
The item is supplied elsewhere within the same declared unit.
Translation compression.
Textual corruption, lacuna, damaged witness, or editorial loss.
Different source text or source version.
Change of speaker, quotation, objection, or hypothetical.
Ordinary incompleteness, oversight, memory lapse, or compositional revision.
The expectation baseline is mistaken or anachronistic.
The investigator has defined the textual boundary incorrectly.
disqualifying_conditions: No independent documentary baseline establishes that the item should be present.
The expected item lies outside the reconstructed scope.
The item is present elsewhere within the same bounded unit.
The absence disappears in the source language or another authoritative witness.
Textual damage, lacuna, editorial loss, or translation fully explains the absence.
The expected item is only inferred from modern doctrinal completeness.
The alleged omission has no material effect on the passage.
The analysis silently imports a preferred doctrine.
The boundary of the relevant discussion is unstable.
The claim depends only on the reader noticing that something was not said.
authorized_engine_action: Preserve the bounded unit and complete context. Create a separate expectation-baseline record before recording the omission. Identify the exact missing item without supplying its doctrinal meaning. Verify absence across relevant witnesses, translations, and textual boundaries. Generate a counterfactual inclusion record without altering the source text. Document the omission's effect on completeness, status, scope, attribution, or force. Search the work for the missing item, parallel treatments, and counterexamples. Evaluate LC-015 separately when the omission occurs inside a quotation. Preserve all ordinary explanations and terminate at candidate status when the baseline or effect remains uncertain.
prohibited_inferences: Do not treat absence as evidence without an expectation baseline.
Do not infer that every unmentioned topic is deliberately omitted.
Do not supply the missing teaching as though it were stated.
Do not infer authorial intention from omission alone.
Do not infer the intended audience or identify knowledgeable readers.
Do not declare the surrounding passage exoteric.
Do not infer doctrinal truth from the omitted item.
Do not collapse LC-014 into LC-015, LC-004, or later sentential-omission techniques.
uncertainty_note: When an item is absent but the expectation baseline, textual scope, witness condition, or material effect remains contestable, preserve a candidate omission with all rival baselines and explanations. Do not infer deliberate silence.

name: Deliberate Misquotation Through Omission
strauss_formulation: A cited statement may omit words that would substantially alter its meaning or disclose its status.
mechanism: A passage presents words as a quotation or identifiable citation of a source, but comparison with the recoverable source shows that one or more words have been omitted. The omitted material changes the quoted proposition, its scope, qualification, attribution, evidentiary status, or doctrinal force. The quotation may therefore appear to support a claim that the fuller source does not support in the same way.
documentary_function: Direct bounded documentary attention under LC-015 without authorizing a concealed-teaching inference.
investigative_requirement: Establish the quotation and its source before evaluating alteration. Recover the most relevant source witness or source-version family; align source and quotation word by word; record every omission without silently normalizing it; reconstruct the proposition and status of the full source and shortened quotation separately; document the material effect; and test abbreviation, memory quotation, alternate source version, translation, grammar, genre, textual corruption, and indirect citation.
distinguished_from: LC-015 requires an identifiable quotation or citation relation. A general silence or missing topic belongs to LC-014.
LC-015 requires source-to-quotation collation. LC-004 compares apparent repetitions within a work and need not involve an external source.
A shortened quotation is not automatically a misquotation. The omission must materially affect meaning, scope, qualification, attribution, status, or doctrinal force.
