An omission may be exact, paraphrastic, translational, or version-dependent. The evaluator must preserve these possibilities rather than assuming a single source text.
The evaluator may corroborate a materially altered quotation, but the word deliberate in the technique name does not authorize automated inference of intention.
The fuller source proposition must not be treated as the author's own teaching, nor may the shortened quotation be rejected solely because it is incomplete.
minimum_trigger_features: A bounded passage presents itself as a quotation, citation, or identifiable reproduction of a source.
A recoverable source or source-version family is identified.
Source and quotation can be aligned at word or phrase level.
At least one source expression is absent from the quotation.
The omission materially changes proposition, scope, qualification, attribution, status, or doctrinal force.
The effect is not merely stylistic abbreviation.
required_corroboration_features: The omitted words explicitly qualify the source as common opinion, report, hypothesis, or limited claim.
The author elsewhere quotes the same source more fully.
A second quotation exhibits the same controlled omission.
The author elsewhere demonstrates knowledge of the omitted wording.
A source comparison shows that the omission changes attribution, modality, quantification, negation, or scope.
Another independently reconstructed literary device converges on the same quotation problem.
The shortened quotation supports a surface claim that the full source materially qualifies.
ordinary_alternatives: Ordinary abbreviation.
Memory quotation.
Paraphrase rather than verbatim quotation.
A different source edition, recension, translation, or textual tradition.
Indirect citation through an intermediary source.
Grammatical adaptation to the new sentence.
Citation convention permitting ellipsis.
Translator or editor omission.
Textual corruption, lacuna, or witness damage.
The omitted words are materially redundant in context.
The source identification is mistaken.
The quotation boundary has been reconstructed incorrectly.
disqualifying_conditions: No identifiable quotation or citation relation exists.
The proposed source cannot be recovered or responsibly bounded.
The quotation matches an attested alternate source version.
No omission remains after reliable collation.
The omission has no material semantic or status effect.
The passage is explicitly presented as summary or paraphrase.
Grammar or translation requires the shortening without changing the proposition.
An editor, translator, copyist, or damaged witness fully explains the omission.
The source attribution is false or unresolved.
The analysis silently modernizes or harmonizes the source wording.
authorized_engine_action: Preserve the quotation, source witness, and complete contexts. Create separate source, quotation, collation, omission, and semantic-effect records. Preserve every source variant and identify the version used for comparison. Normalize full-source and shortened-quotation propositions separately. Test abbreviation, paraphrase, memory, indirect citation, alternate version, translation, grammar, and witness explanations. Search the work for other quotations of the same source and for evidence of source knowledge. Evaluate LC-014 separately for the broader omission relation and LC-004 when internal repetition is independently present. Preserve intentionality as unresolved unless supported by a separate evidence record. Terminate at candidate status when source identity, version, or material effect remains disputed.
prohibited_inferences: Do not call every shortened quotation a misquotation.
Do not infer intention from omission alone.
Do not infer concealment from quotation difference alone.
Do not choose a preferred source version without documentary justification.
Do not treat paraphrase as verbatim quotation.
Do not infer that the fuller source states the author's own teaching.
Do not infer that the shortened quotation is false.
Do not identify the intended reader or hidden teaching.
Do not collapse LC-015 into LC-014 or LC-004.
uncertainty_note: When a source-to-quotation omission is present but source identity, version, quotation status, material effect, or ordinary alternatives remain disputed, preserve a candidate quotation alteration. Do not declare deliberate misquotation.

name: Repetition as Leitmotif
strauss_formulation: Fourfold repetition of an express quotation in a carefully worded book proves that the quotation is something like a leitmotif.
mechanism: An express quotation or recoverable quotation-family recurs at least four times within a carefully bounded work. The recurrence is distributed across distinct contexts or architectonic locations. Exact, altered, and incomplete forms remain separate evidence. The pattern directs attention to the quotation and to the differences among its occurrences without by itself determining what the motif means.
documentary_function: Direct bounded documentary attention under LC-016 without authorizing a concealed-teaching inference.
investigative_requirement: Recover the source quotation; index every occurrence and variant in the declared scope; verify at least four occurrences; preserve exact wording, omissions, additions, and substitutions for each; reconstruct the local and architectonic function of every occurrence; compare the distribution against ordinary repetition, formula, genre, quotation convention, translation, and editorial duplication; and separate the existence of a leitmotif pattern from any interpretation of its significance.
distinguished_from: LC-016 requires recurrent quotation across a work or declared scope. A single repetition pair belongs to LC-004 unless a broader recurrence pattern is independently established.
The source specifically identifies fourfold repetition. This v0.1 evaluator therefore requires at least four recoverable occurrences.
Exact, altered, and incomplete occurrences must not be silently normalized into one text.
Repetition alone is insufficient. Distribution, wording, source relation, and contextual function must be reconstructed.
LC-009 concerns a recurrent terminology system. LC-016 concerns recurrence of a quotation or quotation-family functioning as a motif.
LC-015 concerns omission from a source quotation in a bounded citation. LC-016 may contain altered or incomplete quotations but asks about their work-wide recurrence.
The evaluator may corroborate a leitmotif pattern but may not infer its hidden significance, authorial intention, doctrinal meaning, or concealed teaching.
minimum_trigger_features: A declared work or textual scope is recoverable in stable witnesses.
An express quotation or responsibly bounded quotation-family is identified.
At least four distinct occurrences are recoverable within the declared scope.
Each occurrence is linked documentarily to the same quotation-family.
The occurrences are distributed across more than one local or architectonic context.
The pattern exceeds accidental duplication, formulaic repetition, or mere topic frequency.
required_corroboration_features: The quotation recurs at four or more architectonically distinct locations.
Altered or incomplete occurrences display patterned rather than random variation.
The same omitted or added expression changes function consistently across occurrences.
The author elsewhere comments on or explains the quotation's source.
The motif aligns with chapter boundaries, transitions, or repeated argumentative crises.
A source comparison shows deliberate retention, truncation, or alteration across occurrences.
Another independently reconstructed literary device converges on one or more occurrences.
The occurrence pattern predicts a passage distinction independently verified elsewhere.
ordinary_alternatives: Formulaic repetition.
Liturgical, legal, scholastic, or genre convention.
Ordinary thematic recurrence.
High frequency caused by subject matter.
Repeated citation required by argument.
Memory quotation or ordinary paraphrase.
Translation normalization.
Editorial duplication or harmonization.
Copyist repetition or witness contamination.
Stock phrase or common proverb.
The occurrence index combines unrelated quotation families.
The declared scope is too narrow or selectively sampled.
disqualifying_conditions: Fewer than four distinct occurrences are recoverable.
The occurrences do not belong to the same work or declared scope.
The source or quotation-family relation is unsupported.
The occurrences are confined to one local context or mechanical list.
The recurrence is fully explained by formula, genre, subject matter, or citation necessity.
Variants are silently normalized or reconstructed without evidence.
The pattern disappears after source-language or witness review.
Editorial duplication, copyist error, or textual contamination explains the recurrence.
The analysis depends only on frequency and not on wording, distribution, or function.
The purported motif is assembled from semantically unrelated phrases.
authorized_engine_action: Create a complete occurrence index for the declared scope. Preserve every occurrence and witness independently. Recover the source quotation and source context where possible. Classify exact, altered, incomplete, and unresolved forms separately. Collate every occurrence against the source and record all differences. Map local function and architectonic distribution. Search for missing occurrences, counterexamples, and unrelated repetitions. Test formulaic, generic, thematic, translational, editorial, and witness alternatives. Evaluate LC-004, LC-015, LC-009, LC-017, or LC-020 separately when independently triggered. Preserve motif significance as an open question.
prohibited_inferences: Do not infer a leitmotif from fewer than four occurrences in this v0.1 projection.
Do not infer importance from repetition alone.
Do not silently normalize altered or incomplete quotations.
Do not infer hidden significance from patterned recurrence alone.
Do not infer authorial intention or intended audience.
Do not select a doctrinal interpretation of the motif.
Do not treat every repeated phrase as an express quotation.
Do not collapse LC-016 into LC-004, LC-015, or LC-009.
uncertainty_note: When four or more related occurrences are present but source identity, quotation-family membership, distribution, variant pattern, or ordinary alternatives remain disputed, preserve a candidate leitmotif pattern with all occurrence-level uncertainties. Do not interpret the motif's significance.

name: Rashei Perakim — Beginnings of Chapters
strauss_formulation: The expression may mean not only "chapter headings" but also "beginnings of chapters." Maimonides sometimes gives important hints through the initial word or words of a chapter.
mechanism: The first authorial word or words of a securely bounded chapter occupy a privileged architectonic position. Their lexical content, grammatical form, or relation to other chapter openings may signal the chapter's subject, a division within the work, or a doctrinal relation not stated directly in a heading. The effect must be tested against a complete index of chapter beginnings.
documentary_function: Direct bounded documentary attention under LC-017 without authorizing a concealed-teaching inference.
investigative_requirement: Establish the authorial chapter boundaries and exclude editorial headings, running heads, numerals, and supplied titles. Record the exact first word or words of every chapter in the declared scope; preserve source-language forms and translations; identify the candidate opening's relation to its chapter and the work's architecture; compare all openings for recurrence, contrast, sequence, and exceptional form; and test grammar, transition, genre, translation, and editorial division as ordinary explanations.
distinguished_from: LC-017 concerns the actual opening word or words of a chapter, not merely a chapter title, heading, number, running head, or table-of-contents entry.
A chapter beginning must be authorially or textually grounded. Modern editorial division cannot create an LC-017 signal.
One striking opening may justify a candidate inquiry, but the source requires recording the beginnings of every chapter and testing them against the work's structure.
LC-008 concerns an unobtrusive common word anywhere in a passage. LC-017 concerns the architectonic force of initial chapter placement.
LC-013 concerns mottoes prefixed to a work or part. A motto stands outside ordinary exposition; an LC-017 opening is the first word or phrase of the chapter's exposition.
LC-020 concerns hints generally. LC-017 requires the specific chapter-opening operation.
The evaluator may corroborate a chapter-opening signal but may not infer hidden teaching, intended meaning, authorial intention, audience, or doctrinal truth.
minimum_trigger_features: A chapter boundary is recoverable in a stable witness.
The candidate expression is the actual first authorial word or words of the chapter's exposition.
Editorial headings, numbers, running heads, and supplied titles are excluded.
The opening has a documented lexical, grammatical, thematic, divisional, or doctrinal relation to the chapter or work.
Initial placement materially contributes to the proposed relation.
The claim exceeds the fact that every chapter necessarily has a first word.
required_corroboration_features: Multiple chapter openings form a recoverable lexical or conceptual sequence.
The target opening contrasts systematically with neighboring chapter openings.
The same opening word recurs at architectonically corresponding chapters.
The opening anticipates a division or doctrinal relation developed later in the chapter.
The author elsewhere comments on the opening word or its semantic field.
A source comparison shows controlled preservation, alteration, or repositioning of the opening.
Another independently reconstructed literary device converges on the same chapter boundary.
The opening pattern predicts a structural distinction verified elsewhere in the work.
ordinary_alternatives: Grammatical necessity.
Ordinary connective or transitional wording.
Formulaic chapter opening.
Genre convention.
Editorial chapter division.
Translator-supplied wording.
Running head, chapter number, title, or table-of-contents language.
Scribal paragraphing or later textual segmentation.
Coincidental lexical recurrence.
The opening merely summarizes the chapter's obvious topic.
The investigator selected an arbitrary number of opening words.
The chapter-opening index is incomplete or selectively sampled.
disqualifying_conditions: The chapter boundary is not securely recoverable.
The expression is an editorial heading, numeral, running head, or supplied title.
The alleged opening is not the first authorial word or words of the exposition.
The relation to chapter or work structure is undocumented.
Initial placement adds no material force to the proposed relation.
The pattern disappears in the source language or an authoritative witness.
Editorial division, translation, or later segmentation fully explains the opening.
The chapter-opening index is materially incomplete for the claimed scope.
The analysis depends only on thematic resemblance or the universal fact of initial position.
The selected opening phrase is expanded or contracted until a preferred pattern appears.
authorized_engine_action: Verify chapter boundaries and exclude editorial paratext. Create a complete chapter-opening index for the declared scope. Preserve exact source-language and translated openings separately. Record a fixed extraction rule for the first word or words. Map recurrence, contrast, sequence, exceptional form, and architectonic correspondence. Create a bounded candidate relation for the target opening. Test grammatical, transitional, formulaic, generic, editorial, translational, and segmentation alternatives. Search for counterexamples and openings that do not fit the proposed pattern. Evaluate LC-008, LC-013, LC-016, LC-020, or LC-021 separately when independently triggered. Preserve the opening's intended meaning as unresolved.
prohibited_inferences: Do not treat editorial headings as authorial chapter beginnings.
Do not infer significance from initial position alone.
Do not choose an arbitrary opening-phrase length to manufacture a pattern.
Do not infer a work-wide pattern from a selective sample.
Do not infer hidden teaching, authorial intention, intended audience, or doctrinal truth.
Do not assume the opening simply names the chapter's subject.
Do not silently normalize source-language and translated openings.
Do not collapse LC-017 into LC-008, LC-013, LC-016, or LC-020.
uncertainty_note: When a chapter opening appears architectonically significant but chapter boundaries, editorial status, extraction rule, index completeness, or structural relation remains contested, preserve a candidate opening signal with all rival segmentations and explanations. Do not assign an intended meaning.

name: Rare Statement of the Unconventional Teaching
strauss_formulation: Of two contradictory statements, the statement occurring least frequently is presumptively the one Maimonides considered true.
mechanism: Two proposition-families that contradict one another are inventoried across a declared work or scope. One family occurs less frequently than the other. When the more frequent family is independently documented as conventional and the less frequent family as unconventional, Strauss's rule authorizes a rebuttable presumption favoring the less frequent statement as the candidate the author considered true. The presumption is procedural and does not itself establish truth.
documentary_function: Direct bounded documentary attention under LC-018 without authorizing a concealed-teaching inference.
investigative_requirement: Define the contradictory proposition pair before counting; build a complete occurrence inventory for each family; preserve quotations, paraphrases, implications, speakers, qualifications, modality, and variants; document conventionality and unconventionality independently of frequency; test whether scope, topic, quotation, genre, pedagogy, textual loss, or classification bias explains the count difference; and apply Strauss's presumption only after contradiction, completeness, and unequal frequency have survived adversarial review.
distinguished_from: LC-018 is not a general rule that rare statements are true. It applies to two documented contradictory statement-families within a declared scope.
The less frequent statement need not occur only once; it must occur fewer times than its contradictory counterpart.
Frequency must be counted by proposition-family, not by raw word recurrence.
Conventionality and unconventionality require independent historical, doctrinal, audience, or intra-work evidence. They may not be inferred from frequency alone.
LC-019 describes frequent repetition of the conventional view. LC-018 supplies the corresponding rule of discernment favoring the rarer contradictory statement.
LC-002 concerns unequal prominence between two contradictory statements. LC-018 concerns unequal frequency across the declared work or scope.
LC-003 concerns contradiction by implication. Implicational occurrences may enter the frequency index only when each reconstruction is separately documented.
The evaluator may record that Strauss's presumption is applicable, but it may not convert that presumption into adjudicated doctrinal truth or final authorial preference.
minimum_trigger_features: Two bounded proposition-families are documentarily reconstructed.
The proposition-families contradict one another under the same relevant scope, subject, modality, and attribution.
A complete or explicitly bounded occurrence inventory exists for both families.
Both families occur at least once.
The occurrence counts are unequal.
The less frequent family is independently documented as unconventional and the more frequent family as conventional.
The count difference is not produced merely by raw lexical frequency or selective sampling.
required_corroboration_features: The less frequent statement occurs once while the contradictory conventional statement recurs many times.
The author marks the rarer statement with a hint, contradiction, qualification, or architectonic signal.
The frequent statement is attributed to common opinion, the vulgar, law, custom, or an exoteric audience.
The rarer statement is placed in a low-prominence or difficult location.
The author elsewhere distinguishes the two views in a way compatible with the frequency pattern.
LC-019 independently confirms systematic repetition of the conventional family.
Another independently reconstructed literary device converges on the rarer statement.
The frequency pattern remains stable across authoritative witnesses and translations.
ordinary_alternatives: The more frequent view is discussed more because it is the chapter's subject.
The rarer view is a quotation, objection, hypothetical, or report rather than an authorial statement.
The proposition-families are not genuinely contradictory.
The statements differ in scope, modality, time, condition, definition, or speaker.
The occurrence index is incomplete or selectively sampled.
Paraphrases or implications are classified inconsistently.
Genre, pedagogy, legal exposition, or historical narration explains the frequency difference.
Textual loss, witness variation, translation, or editorial segmentation alters the counts.
The conventionality classification is anachronistic or unsupported.
One family is lexically easier to recognize than the other.
Compositional revision or multiple source layers explain the distribution.
Frequency reflects topic importance rather than concealment.
disqualifying_conditions: No contradictory proposition pair is established.
Either proposition-family has no occurrence.
The occurrence counts are equal.
The inventory is materially incomplete for the claimed scope.
The less frequent family is not independently documented as unconventional.
The more frequent family is not independently documented as conventional.
The contradiction disappears after qualification, modality, scope, or attribution is restored.
The count difference results from raw-word counting rather than proposition classification.
Quotation, objection, hypothetical, or speaker differences fully explain the pattern.
Textual loss, witness variation, translation, or editorial intervention makes the counts unstable.
The investigator chose the scope after seeing the desired frequency result.
authorized_engine_action: Create two independent proposition-family records. Create and test a formal contradiction relation. Build a complete occurrence index for both families. Preserve direct, paraphrastic, implicational, quoted, hypothetical, and reported occurrences separately. Document inclusion and exclusion rules before final counting. Record conventionality and unconventionality evidence independently of frequency. Calculate counts and identify the less frequent family. Test all scope, attribution, genre, pedagogy, textual, translation, and classification alternatives. Record Strauss's presumption as applicable only after all trigger conditions are met. Evaluate LC-019 separately for the frequent conventional family and adjacent contradiction techniques when independently triggered. Preserve the presumption as rebuttable and distinct from doctrinal truth.
prohibited_inferences: Do not infer that any rare statement is true.
Do not apply the rule without a documented contradictory pair.
Do not infer conventionality or unconventionality from frequency alone.
Do not count raw words as proposition occurrences.
Do not combine different speakers, modalities, scopes, or conditions without evidence.
Do not treat reconstructed implications as direct quotations.
Do not select the less frequent statement as adjudicated doctrinal truth.
Do not infer concealment, intention, or intended audience from frequency alone.
Do not collapse LC-018 into LC-019, LC-002, or LC-003.
uncertainty_note: When a contradictory pair and unequal counts are present but inventory completeness, proposition-family classification, conventionality, attribution, or ordinary alternatives remain disputed, preserve a candidate rarity presumption with both counts and all disputed occurrences. Do not select doctrinal truth.

name: Frequent Repetition of the Conventional View
strauss_formulation: Views suitable to or accepted by the vulgar are repeated as frequently as possible.
mechanism: Within a declared work or scope, a proposition-family independently documented as conventional recurs more frequently and more broadly than its contradictory counterpart. The conventional family is repeated across distinct contexts, speakers, or architectonic locations in a way that increases familiarity and surface authority. The pattern is evaluated separately from LC-018's presumption concerning the rarer statement.
documentary_function: Direct bounded documentary attention under LC-019 without authorizing a concealed-teaching inference.
investigative_requirement: Define the conventional proposition-family and its contradictory counterpart before counting; inventory every occurrence of both families; document conventionality independently of frequency; map every relevant opportunity for repetition within the declared scope; test whether the conventional view is restated across those opportunities; and exclude topic necessity, pedagogy, genre, quotation, legal exposition, translation, witness variation, and classification bias before treating recurrence as a concealment-related pattern.
distinguished_from: LC-019 identifies the repeated conventional surface-teaching pattern. LC-018 uses the unequal-frequency pair to apply a rebuttable presumption to the rarer statement.
A frequent statement is not conventional merely because it is frequent. Conventionality requires independent historical, doctrinal, audience, or intra-work evidence.
A conventional statement is not false merely because it is repeated. LC-019 does not select truth or authorial preference.
Raw lexical repetition is insufficient. Counting occurs at the level of documented proposition-families.
The phrase 'as frequently as possible' requires a bounded opportunity analysis, not merely a large absolute count.
LC-016 concerns recurrence of a quotation as leitmotif. LC-019 concerns repeated assertion of a conventional proposition-family.
LC-009 concerns recurrent terminology. LC-019 concerns repeated propositions, even when expressed with different words.
The evaluator may corroborate a conventional-repetition pattern but may not declare the frequent view exoteric, false, insincere, or intentionally protective.
minimum_trigger_features: A conventional proposition-family and a contradictory counterpart are documentarily reconstructed.
The contradiction holds under aligned subject, scope, modality, qualification, and attribution.
Both proposition-families occur at least once in the declared scope.
The conventional family occurs more frequently than its contradictory counterpart.
The conventional family recurs across more than one local or architectonic context.
Conventionality is documented independently of frequency.
A bounded repetition-opportunity analysis shows that the conventional view is repeatedly restated where it could relevantly recur.
The recurrence exceeds raw-word frequency, accidental duplication, and selective sampling.
required_corroboration_features: The conventional family recurs at architectonically distinct locations.
The conventional family is explicitly attributed to common opinion, custom, law, the vulgar, or an exterior audience.
Different formulations preserve the same conventional proposition across contexts.
The contradictory family remains rare while the conventional family is repeatedly restated.
The conventional family appears at multiple relevant repetition opportunities.
LC-018 independently confirms the unequal-frequency contradictory pair.
A hint, omission, contradiction, or placement device converges on the rarer counterpart.
The repetition pattern remains stable across authoritative witnesses and translations.
ordinary_alternatives: The conventional view is the main subject of the work or chapter.
Pedagogical repetition for clarity or memory.
Legal, scholastic, liturgical, or genre convention.
Repeated quotation of an external authority.
Historical narration of a commonly held view.
The frequent family contains materially different propositions incorrectly merged together.
The rare family contains unrecognized paraphrases or implications.
Speaker, audience, scope, modality, or condition differences explain the distribution.
Translation normalization makes distinct formulations appear identical.
Editorial duplication, witness contamination, or textual repetition.
The opportunity map is incomplete or constructed after seeing the result.
Compositional revision or multiple source layers explain the recurrence.
Frequency reflects topic importance rather than surface protection.
disqualifying_conditions: No contradictory proposition-family is documented.
Either family has no occurrence.
The conventional family does not occur more frequently than the counterpart.
The frequent family is not independently documented as conventional.
The recurrence is confined to one local passage, list, or mechanical sequence.
No bounded repetition-opportunity analysis exists.
The recurrence is fully explained by topic, pedagogy, genre, law, quotation, or narration.
The occurrence inventory is materially incomplete or selectively sampled.
The contradiction disappears after scope, modality, qualification, or attribution is restored.
Raw lexical counts substitute for proposition-family classification.
Translation, editorial duplication, witness variation, or contamination makes the frequency pattern unstable.
The declared scope was chosen after counting to manufacture dominance.
authorized_engine_action: Create independent conventional and contrary proposition-family records. Create and test a formal contradiction relation. Build complete occurrence inventories for both families. Preserve direct, paraphrastic, implicational, quoted, hypothetical, and reported occurrences separately. Document conventionality independently of frequency. Create a repetition-opportunity map before final evaluation. Calculate counts, distribution, and opportunity coverage under predeclared rules. Test topic, pedagogy, genre, legal, quotation, translation, witness, classification, and revision alternatives. Evaluate LC-018 separately for the rarity presumption concerning the counterpart. Preserve truth, sincerity, exotericism, intention, and audience as unresolved.
prohibited_inferences: Do not infer conventionality from frequency alone.
Do not infer falsity from frequent repetition.
Do not infer truth from rarity within LC-019.
Do not treat raw word recurrence as proposition repetition.
Do not merge materially different propositions to increase the count.
Do not ignore unrecognized paraphrases or implications of the contrary family.
Do not infer exotericism, insincerity, concealment, intention, or intended audience.
Do not collapse LC-019 into LC-018, LC-016, or LC-009.
Do not claim 'as frequently as possible' without a bounded opportunity analysis.
uncertainty_note: When the conventional family dominates the count but proposition-family boundaries, opportunity coverage, conventionality, attribution, or ordinary alternatives remain disputed, preserve a candidate conventional-repetition pattern with all disputed occurrences and opportunities. Do not declare the view false or exoteric.

name: Hint
strauss_formulation: A small textual indication may assist the attentive reader in discovering a contradiction or determining which contradictory statement is true.
mechanism: A small, bounded textual feature directs an attentive reader toward an independently documentable contradiction or toward evidence bearing on how two contradictory statements should be weighed. The feature does not state the result directly. Its operation is reconstructed through an explicit, finite discovery path from cue to target.
documentary_function: Direct bounded documentary attention under LC-020 without authorizing a concealed-teaching inference.
investigative_requirement: Preserve the exact cue before interpreting it; define the contradiction or discernment problem independently of the cue; record every inferential step connecting cue to target; show how the cue materially narrows or redirects the inquiry; test whether ordinary syntax, reference, rhetoric, topic, translation, textual corruption, editorial intervention, or another already defined technique explains the feature; and reject paths that depend on unbounded association or hindsight.
distinguished_from: LC-020 requires a bounded discovery path. A small textual feature is not a hint merely because it appears unusual or suggestive.
The contradiction or competing-statement problem must be independently documented. The hint may assist discovery or discernment but may not create the target problem by itself.
A hint need not disclose a proposition directly. It must nevertheless materially narrow, redirect, or structure a recoverable inquiry.
Local function labels such as contradiction-discovery or statement-discernment are evaluator aids, not canonical Straussian subtypes.
LC-021 and LC-022 may supply hint-like cues, but inappropriate expression and clumsy transition remain separately evaluated possible devices.
LC-008, LC-013, LC-017, and LC-016 may converge with LC-020. Their own documentary operations must be established independently.
A hint path may support application of LC-018, but LC-020 does not itself select which contradictory statement is true.
The evaluator may corroborate a cue-to-inquiry path but may not infer hidden teaching, intended audience, authorial intention, or doctrinal truth.
minimum_trigger_features: A bounded textual cue is recoverable in a stable witness.
An independently documented contradiction or contradictory-statement discernment problem exists.
A finite sequence of evidence-bearing inferential steps connects the cue to that target.
The cue materially narrows, redirects, or structures the inquiry.
The path does not depend solely on free association, perceived suggestiveness, or hindsight.
The cue does not state the proposed result directly.
required_corroboration_features: The cue points to a contradiction independently established elsewhere in the work.
The cue aligns with a separately documented LC-018 or LC-019 frequency pattern.
A parallel cue produces the same kind of bounded discovery path elsewhere.
The author later confirms a distinction reached through the cue.
The cue occurs at an architectonically marked location.
A source comparison shows controlled retention, alteration, omission, or placement of the cue.
Another independently reconstructed literary device converges on the same target.
Removing the cue materially weakens or obscures the documented discovery path.
ordinary_alternatives: Ordinary syntax or connective function.
Simple cross-reference.
Topical transition.
Rhetorical emphasis.
Pedagogical reminder.
Lexical ambiguity without a bounded target.
Common-word usage without independent trigger.
Editorial heading, punctuation, or annotation.
Translation artifact.
Textual corruption or witness variation.
Coincidental resemblance.
A fully explicit statement rather than a hint.
Investigator hindsight or confirmation bias.
An unbounded chain of associations.
disqualifying_conditions: No exact cue can be isolated.
No independent contradiction or discernment problem is documented.
The proposed path contains unsupported inferential steps.
The cue does not materially narrow or redirect inquiry.
The path can point equally well to many unrelated targets.
The cue states the proposed result directly.
Ordinary syntax, cross-reference, rhetoric, pedagogy, translation, or textual variation fully explains the feature.
The path appears only after the investigator has already selected the desired conclusion.
The cue-target relation disappears in the source language or an authoritative witness.
Another technique fully accounts for the feature and no distinct hint operation remains.
authorized_engine_action: Preserve the cue and full local context. Create the target contradiction or discernment record independently. Create an ordered discovery-path record with evidence for every step. Document how the cue narrows, redirects, or structures inquiry. Generate and test alternative paths and targets. Review source language, translation, variants, speaker, and architecture. Evaluate LC-008, LC-013, LC-017, LC-018, LC-019, LC-021, or LC-022 separately when independently triggered. Open only a bounded inquiry supported by the documented path. Preserve the teaching, intention, audience, and truth question as unresolved.
prohibited_inferences: Do not treat every small textual feature as a hint.
Do not infer a hint from suggestiveness alone.
Do not create the target contradiction from the cue itself.
Do not use an unlimited associative chain.
Do not omit unsupported path steps.
Do not infer intended audience from the difficulty of recognizing the hint.
Do not infer authorial intention, concealment, hidden teaching, or doctrinal truth.
Do not use LC-020 to bypass the evidentiary requirements of LC-018.
Do not collapse LC-020 into the device that supplies the cue.
uncertainty_note: When a bounded cue and target problem exist but one or more discovery steps, alternative targets, material guidance, or ordinary explanations remain disputed, preserve a candidate hint path with every supported and unsupported step marked. Do not select a teaching or truth.

name: Inappropriate Expression
strauss_formulation: A seemingly ill-fitting expression may function as a stumbling block that directs attention beneath the surface, but it may also be merely inappropriate.
mechanism: A bounded expression conflicts with an independently documented lexical, grammatical, semantic, register, pragmatic, technical, or contextual fit baseline. The mismatch materially interrupts the surface reading and may function as a stumbling block that authorizes a bounded question about why this expression occurs here. The anomaly remains weaker evidence than a contradiction and may prove to be merely inappropriate.
documentary_function: Direct bounded documentary attention under LC-021 without authorizing a concealed-teaching inference.
investigative_requirement: Preserve the exact expression; establish the fit baseline before calling it inappropriate; document the mismatch under historically and linguistically appropriate standards; verify source language, grammar, translation, speaker, genre, technical vocabulary, and witnesses; formulate only a bounded textual question; test every ordinary repair and explanation; and require independent corroboration before treating the expression as more than a possible signal.
distinguished_from: An unusual, inelegant, or disliked expression is not automatically inappropriate. A documented fit baseline is required.
The expression may be merely inappropriate. LC-021 therefore authorizes weaker evidentiary force than an established contradiction.
The authorized response is a bounded textual question, not an inferred hidden answer.
LC-006 concerns multiple lexical meanings. LC-021 concerns a documented mismatch between an expression and its expected local or technical fit.
LC-008 concerns concealment in an unobtrusive common word. LC-021 requires the expression to operate as a noticeable stumbling block.
LC-020 requires a finite cue-to-target discovery path. LC-021 may supply a possible cue, but it does not itself establish that path.
LC-022 concerns an awkward transition between subjects rather than the fit of a bounded expression.
The evaluator may corroborate an inappropriate-expression signal but may not infer hidden meaning, authorial intention, intended audience, concealment, or doctrinal truth.

name: Clumsy Transition
strauss_formulation: An awkward movement between subjects may signal a concealed relationship or structural break, but it may also be mere clumsiness unless corroborated.
mechanism: Two adjacent or explicitly linked textual units move from one subject, claim, level, speaker, or argumentative task to another in a way that conflicts with an independently documented transition-coherence baseline. The mismatch materially interrupts the expected progression and may direct attention to a possible relation, division, or structural break between the units. The transition remains weak evidence and may be mere clumsiness.
documentary_function: Direct bounded documentary attention under LC-022 without authorizing a concealed-teaching inference.
investigative_requirement: Preserve the full units on both sides of the transition; identify the exact transition boundary and connective language; reconstruct the preceding and following subjects independently; establish a historically, linguistically, and generically appropriate coherence baseline; document the mismatch and its material structural effect; formulate only a bounded question about relation or break; test ellipsis, ordinary topic change, pedagogy, genre, speaker shift, source compilation, translation, textual damage, and compositional revision; and require independent corroboration before strengthening the signal.
distinguished_from: LC-022 concerns the relation between adjacent textual units or subjects. LC-021 concerns the fit of a bounded expression within its local context.
An abrupt topic change is not automatically clumsy. A documented transition-coherence baseline and material mismatch are required.
A clumsy transition may indicate a possible relation or structural break, but it does not identify that relation by itself.
The authorized response is a bounded structural question, not an inferred hidden answer.
LC-005 concerns an intermediary assertion and creeping contradiction. LC-022 concerns awkward movement between units even when no contradiction exists.
LC-017 may involve a chapter boundary. LC-022 applies only when the movement across that boundary is independently shown to be awkward or structurally anomalous.
LC-020 may use a clumsy transition as a cue, but a separate finite cue-to-target path is required before LC-020 is satisfied.
The evaluator may corroborate a transition anomaly but may not infer a hidden relationship, authorial intention, intended audience, concealment, or doctrinal truth.
minimum_trigger_features: A bounded transition between two adjacent or explicitly linked textual units is recoverable in a stable witness.
The subject or argumentative task on each side is independently reconstructed.
An independent and historically appropriate transition-coherence baseline is documented.
The actual transition conflicts with that baseline in a specific way.
The mismatch materially interrupts expected continuity or marks a structural break.
A bounded question about relation or division can be formulated without asserting a hidden answer.
The anomaly exceeds a simple, explicit, and adequately signaled topic change.
required_corroboration_features: A later passage explicitly supplies the missing relation between the two subjects.
A parallel passage uses a smoother transition that clarifies the same structural relation.
The same subjects are juxtaposed elsewhere at an architectonically corresponding location.
The transition converges with an independently documented contradiction, hint, chapter-opening signal, or omission.
The anomaly is preserved across authoritative witnesses and translations.
A source comparison shows controlled juxtaposition, deletion, repositioning, or connective alteration.
Restoring an ordinary connective or omitted intermediate step materially changes the structural reading.
The transition occurs at a marked division, chapter boundary, or argumentative crisis.
ordinary_alternatives: Ordinary topic change.
Ellipsis of an obvious intermediate step.

Ordinary revision, haste, or stylistic clumsiness.
The coherence baseline is mistaken, anachronistic, or too rigid.
The investigator has reconstructed the subjects or boundary incorrectly.
disqualifying_conditions: No stable transition boundary can be isolated.
The two textual units are not adjacent or explicitly linked.
The subjects or argumentative tasks on either side are not independently reconstructed.
No independent transition-coherence baseline is documented.
The shift is explicitly and adequately signaled.
The mismatch depends only on stylistic taste or modern expectations.
The transition has no material structural effect.
Speaker, quotation, genre, pedagogy, ellipsis, translation, paragraphing, or textual variation fully explains the movement.
The bounded question already assumes a concealed relation or break.
The anomaly disappears after source-language syntax or the correct textual boundary is restored.
Another technique fully accounts for the feature and no distinct LC-022 operation remains.
authorized_engine_action: Preserve both textual units, the transition boundary, and complete context. Create separate subject or argumentative-task records for each side. Create a transition-coherence baseline before classifying awkwardness. Create a typed mismatch and structural-effect record. Formulate a bounded question about possible relation or structural break. Test ellipsis, ordinary topic change, pedagogy, genre, speaker shift, source compilation, translation, editorial segmentation, textual damage, and revision. Compare authorial transition practice and parallel passages. Search for independent corroboration before escalating the signal. Evaluate LC-005, LC-017, LC-020, or LC-021 separately when independently triggered. Preserve the possibility of mere clumsiness.
prohibited_inferences: Do not infer significance from abruptness alone.
Do not use stylistic dislike as a coherence baseline.
Do not infer a concealed relation merely because two subjects are adjacent.
Do not infer a structural break without a documented material effect.
Do not treat LC-022 as equivalent in force to a contradiction.
Do not compel deep inquiry without independent corroboration.
Do not infer hidden meaning, authorial intention, intended audience, concealment, or doctrinal truth.
Do not silently insert a missing transition into the source text.
Do not collapse LC-022 into LC-005, LC-017, LC-020, or LC-021.
uncertainty_note: When a documented transition mismatch exists but the coherence baseline, subject reconstruction, structural effect, or ordinary explanations remain disputed, preserve a candidate clumsy-transition signal and the bounded question it raises. Preserve explicitly the possibility of mere clumsiness.
